format.environment: Google Meet, screen share your IDE or shared coding link

problems.name: Two Sum (Sorted)
Max Subarray Sum of Size K
problems.solution:
def max_subarray_sum(nums: list[int], k: int) -> int:
    window_sum = sum(nums[:k])
    max_sum = window_sum
    for i in range(k, len(nums)):
        window_sum += nums[i] - nums[i - k]
        max_sum = max(max_sum, window_sum)
    return max_sum

problems.name: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
Find Next Biggest Number from Digits
Min Stack
Stack using Queues
Climbing Stairs
Maximum Subarray
Zigzag Level Order Traversal

sample_questions: What do you know about SQL injection? How to defend?
Explain the difference between hashing and encryption
Explain database indexing and difference between isolation levels
How would you handle concurrent updates to the same row?

focus_areas: Problem solving - Challenges faced and how you solved them
Scale - How the system handles load
prepare_for: Walk me through your most complex project
Describe a challenge you faced and how you solved it
What would you do differently if you rebuilt it?

what_they_look_for: Problem-solving approach - Think out loud, clarify requirements
Code quality - Clean, readable, handles edge cases
Complexity analysis - Know time/space complexity of your solution
Technical breadth - Understanding of backend systems
Communication - Clear explanation of your thought process
common_mistakes: Jumping to code immediately - Clarify requirements, discuss approach first
Silent coding - Think out loud, explain your decisions
Ignoring edge cases - Always ask about constraints and edge cases
Not testing - Walk through your code with examples
Overcomplicating - Start simple, optimize if asked
tips: Focus on medium LeetCode, hard is rare
Interviewer was helpful and gave hints when stuck
Be ready to discuss your GitHub repos
Database internals questions are common for backend
